items.headline: Why Did Albemarle (NYSE:ALB) Gain Market Attention This Week? - Kalkine Media
items.snippet: Albemarle (NYSE:ALB) moved into the current market conversation after the company announced a quarterly common stock dividend as lithium and specialty materials remained in focus.

items.headline: Will ALB's Expansion Actions Power Stronger Sales Volumes?
items.snippet: Albemarle Corporation ALB is strategically executing its projects aimed at boosting its global lithium conversion capacity. The market for lithium batteries and energy storage remains strong.

items.headline: Albemarle Stock Loses 25% in a Month: What Should Investors Do Now?
items.snippet: Albemarle Corporation's ALB shares have tumbled 25.5% in the past month, underperforming the Zacks Chemical - Diversified industry and the S&P 500's declines of 6.7% and 0.1%, respectively. Falling lithium market prices are weighing on the ALB stock lately.

items.headline: [Nonferrous Morning Briefing] Supply-demand imbalance may return, lithium prices plummet
items.snippet: US-based Albemarle (ALB). To expand production capacity, the government is actively promoting a development model that combines state-owned enterprises with private projects. (Shanghai Metals Market)

items.headline: Lithium Is on the Rise Again. Does That Make Albemarle a Buy?
items.snippet: The price of lithium is bouncing back. After lithium prices collapsed from their 2022 peaks, the market finally found its floor in late 2024 and has staged a resilient year-to-date rebound. Chinese spot prices have climbed back into the $23 per kilogram (kg) range.

items.headline: Energy storage demand grows 40% annually, lithium producers bet on growth drivers beyond electric vehicles
items.snippet: Albemarle (ALB.US), the world's largest lithium producer, also noted that demand for its energy storage business is steadily growing, contrasting with the irregularity of demand from electric vehicles.
